text:
Deep learning has been rapidly evolving in the recent years and has been extended to non-Euclidean domains such as graph-structured data. Graphs are a rich data format for encoding complex relational information between different elements, and has thus become a standard data format in many modern machine learning applications. This work is concerned with the study of node and edge detection in various contexts such as text, entities and relations to construct functional graphs and study the GNN that is suitable for a given application.

Since graph neural networks (GNNs) are capable of enabling message passing in graphs, nodes can communicate with each other to exploit the complex vertex and edge relationships. This work examines some popular GNNs, such as Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs), GraphSAGE and Graph Attention Networks (GATs) in terms of their message passing techniques, their merits and demerits and how they perform in different scenarios. In addition, this paper provides a summary of the common GNN datasets, Python libraries and their real-world applications. This review is intended to provide a more comprehensive resource for researchers and practitioners working on GNNs.
